is like workforce AI literacy.

Teach all the employees how to prompt, and that's usually for writing and research, maybe image generation, where something personal you're working on, for the most part, maybe you create a little agent and share it with some colleagues, but it's largely around, hey, I got this thing I got to do, AI can do it faster, teach me how to think that way and how to be productive using AI as a new tool in my tool belt, right?

And we see this, we have a great, great, great training program that we've done, we've done this kind of joint partnership with this company called Pair, and the thing with AI that's so important is you have to make it personal, right?
If I'm in HR, and we demo a bunch of sales and marketing things, then this isn't for HR.
If I'm in sales and marketing, I demo a bunch of legal stuff, it's not for sales and marketing.

So, each individual person,

I was in, here's my role, here's my industry, here's my objectives for the year, and then the hands-on training is customized to that person, giving them real-world industry exercises to prompt and practice with.

And so that really gets folks going in, and this is, you know, Erik, this is the other challenge, right?

Is, those of us who hang out in the AI world is such a small part of the population, estimated five to 7% of any company is kind of a first responder, early adopter in AI, and the other 90 plus percent, man, they're working a 50 hour a week, they've got Cub Scouts and softball and all kinds of other crap going on in their life, and they don't give two  about who all the different LLMs are, and which one I should use versus another one.
And, you know, you know, if it's not accessible.
It just doesn't happen, right?

If they don't want it, there's not a lot we can do, but if they promote it, they incent it, they, you know, gamify it in some way within their organization, it goes really well.
So that's the first kind of pillar to what we see.

They're tired of negotiating all these contracts.
They're scared to death to pick one and pick the wrong one.

We're going to go with Gemini and then, you know, it fizzles out.
We're to with Wama and, you know, ugh, right?

We have it.

We have it.

And those two factors largely outweigh.

If you're dying to use Cloud 4.5, do.
